definition: First beat of the cycle and the main resolution point.
The waved (empty) vibhag used as a structural marker.
A clapped vibhag marker showing stress points in the taal.
Single beat unit in a taal cycle.
Grouping of matras inside one avartan.
One complete cycle from sam to next sam.
Canonical bol pattern that identifies a taal.
Tempo: vilambit, madhya, or drut.
Subdivisional play against a fixed pulse.
Phrase repeated three times to resolve on sam.
Short fixed composition, often energetic and cadential.
A three-round structure where each round itself contains a tihai design.
Introductory improvisational form, usually in vilambit teental.
Theme-and-variation compositional form with rule-bound bol grammar.
Fast flowing composition, often with dense bol movement.
Right-hand treble drum of the tabla pair.
Left-hand bass drum of the tabla pair.
Central black tuning paste that defines pitch and timbre.
Outer ring area used for bright rim strokes.
Middle playing area between syahi and kinar.
